request.question:
Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.
In plain English: can we deliver chemotherapy more precisely to cancer cells by attaching it to a targeting molecule?

hypothesis.intervention: conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles
hypothesis.threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin and reducing cytotoxicity by at least 40% in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready today.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: doxorubicin folate PLGA HeLa intracellular accumulation cytotoxicity

In plain English: can we deliver chemotherapy more precisely to cancer cells by attaching it to a targeting molecule?

Although the biological system of HeLa cells is not directly addressed in the papers, the general type of cancer cells used suggests relevance. The experimental type is similar as they involve drug delivery studies; hence, the similarities across intervention and experiment type categorize it as similar work.

plan.objective: To evaluate the intracellular accumulation of doxorubicin when conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles compared to free doxorubicin in HeLa cells.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be treated with folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles loaded with doxorubicin and free doxorubicin as a control. The intracellular drug accumulation will be quantified using fluorescence microscopy, and cytotoxicity will be assessed in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells.
controls: Doxorubicin at a concentration of 10 µM in culture medium (e.g. DMEM with 10% FBS).
Untreated HEK293 cells with no doxorubicin exposure.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Nanoparticle formulation
Dissolve 100 mg of PLGA (lactide:glycolide ratio 50:50, Sigma Aldrich) in 3 mL of dichloromethane (DCM) at room temperature.
Expected outcome: PLGA is fully dissolved in DCM.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Nanoparticle formulation
Add 10 mg of doxorubicin hydrochloride (dissolved in 500 µL of DMSO) to the PLGA solution and stir for 1 h at room temperature.
Expected outcome: Doxorubicin is uniformly incorporated into the PLGA solution.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 10 min | Nanoparticle formulation
Emulsify the PLGA-doxorubicin solution in 10 mL of 2% polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) solution at 5000 rpm for 5 min using a homogenizer (Ultra-Turrax T25).
Expected outcome: Formation of a stable emulsion.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 2 h | Nanoparticle formulation
Allow the emulsion to evaporate DCM under reduced pressure for 2 h using a rotary evaporator.
Expected outcome: DCM is evaporated, leading to nanoparticle formation.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Particle characterization
Resuspend the nanoparticles in 1 mL of phosphate buffered saline (PBS) for characterization using dynamic light scattering (DLS) to measure particle size.
Expected outcome: Nanoparticles should have a size range of 100-200 nm.

DAY 1 | 24 h | Cell culture
Seed 1 × 10^5 HeLa cells in a 6-well plate with 2 mL of DMEM containing 10% FBS and incubate at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 24 h.
Expected outcome: HeLa cells should adhere and proliferate satisfactorily.

DAY 1 | 4 h | Drug treatment
Treat HeLa cells for 4 h with either folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles loaded with doxorubicin (50 µg/mL) or free doxorubicin (10 µM).
Expected outcome: Cellular uptake of doxorubicin confirmed by fluorescence microscopy.

DAY 2 | 24 h | Cytotoxicity assay
Wash HEK293 cells (1 × 10^5 cells) with PBS and treat with free doxorubicin (10 µM) or no treatment for 24 h, then assess cell viability using MTT assay.
Expected outcome: Cell viability should show at least a 40% reduction compared to untreated controls.

DAY 3 | 2 h | Data analysis
Quantify intracellular doxorubicin accumulation using fluorescence intensity measured via flow cytometry and perform statistical analysis using Student's t-test.
Expected outcome: Intracellular accumulation shows at least a 3-fold increase in HeLa cells compared to controls.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Folate receptors are overexpressed in HeLa cells facilitating endocytosis of folate-functionalized nanoparticles.
Doxorubicin retains its cytotoxic activity when encapsulated in nanoparticles.
PLGA nanoparticles are stable under cellular conditions and effectively release doxorubicin.
decision_criteria: Confirm if intracellular doxorubicin accumulation is ≥ 3-fold higher in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
Evaluate if cytotoxicity in HEK293 cells drops ≥ 40% post-treatment with doxorubicin.
Ensure nanoparticle size is within the range of 100-200 nm for optimal cellular uptake.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of PLGA and doxorubicin across experiments.
Standardize cell passage number for HeLa and HEK293 cells to ≤ 10.
Schedule calibration for flow cytometer prior to analysis.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin and reducing cytotoxicity by at least 40% in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To evaluate the intracellular accumulation of doxorubicin when conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles compared to free doxorubicin in HeLa cells.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
